asset_sources.urdf_uri: g1_description/g1_23dof_mode_10.urdf (on-robot) — actuated joints match the live SDK; the 29-DOF g1_29dof_*.urdf is the kinematic superset
asset_sources.notes: The real robot is a 23-DOF G1 EDU. unitree_ros / g1_description ships both the 23-DOF and 29-DOF descriptions; this descriptor records the 23-DOF as-built robot.

ee_links.notes: On the 23-DOF arm the distal ACTUATED link is the forearm (wrist_roll); there is no wrist_pitch/wrist_yaw. The palm is a fixed offset from this link, so arm_fk reports a correct palm XYZ. The sim 29-DOF asset's EE convention is *_wrist_yaw_link (see sim_asset).

hands.control: Modbus over a single FTDI FT4232H quad USB-UART -> TCP JSON bridge (see unitree/g1/brainco_touch). Left hand Modbus 0x7e, right 0x7f; port assignment drifts, always probe.

driver.install: BUILT FROM SOURCE via depth_camera_sight/install_pyrealsense2.sh into /home/unitree/librealsense (NOT pip: the PyPI wheel needs GLIBC 2.32 but the EDU ships 2.31, so a pip install imports then fails).
driver.runtime: source depth_camera_sight/setup_env.sh before use (sets PYTHONPATH/LD_LIBRARY_PATH/LD_PRELOAD) — a bare 'import pyrealsense2' fails without it. RGB needs no extra deps (Unitree VideoClient over DDS).

calibration.notes: Tilt 51.29 deg DOWN, mount height ~1.242 m, 0.83 cm RMS over ~18k floor pixels. At this pitch the camera sees the floor + near surface, NOT the room (a mattress edge is still resolvable 2-3 m out in the upper frame). This single number sets the sim CameraCfg pitch — measured, not guessed. Re-measure per robot.
1920x1080 over the Unitree video hub (DDS). Mounted ~1 cm left of the depth module; NOT pixel-aligned with depth. Feeds the DINOv2 vision sidecar.

occlusions.description: Face-frame vertical bars blank ±40-45 deg forward azimuth — 0 points in those sectors.
Lower jaw / chin blanks everything below -10 deg elevation, hiding the far floor (a near surface at ~-7 deg is still seen).
Dome housing self-returns cluster at the sensor origin; dropped inside a 0.15 m radius.

calibration.method: table-top z-sign + side-object y-sign probes on hardware (lidar_sight)
calibration.notes: The MID-360 is physically mounted UPSIDE DOWN in the crown; a +180 deg roll correction recovers clean +x-forward/+y-left/+z-up body frame. LiDAR-derived torso height (0.804 m) agrees with the camera's tape+SVD (0.812 m) to 0.8 cm. Detect a bed via find_tables: height-band filter + dominant horizontal plane + planar-area threshold.

compute.notes: Enumerated LIVE on the robot (discover_compute.py via the vision-sidecar CUDA runtime): torch device name 'Orin', device_count=1, 15.0 GiB CUDA-accessible unified memory. Hosts the vision sidecar.

The G1 has a rear expansion port for an optional Jetson Thor — a SEPARATE compute node (own CPU+GPU+OS), NOT a cuda:N on the Orin. Not attached on this unit (probe returns present=false). When installed, run a vision sidecar on the Thor and set 'host' to its address; clients target host:9878 (the sidecar binds 0.0.0.0 and takes VISION_SIDECAR_HOST/PORT/DEVICE).

speaker.notes: TtsMaker verified speaking on the EDU; PlayStream for bring-your-own PCM; SetVolume sets the PERSISTENT master gain. See unitree/g1/voice.

microphone.notes: Unitree engineering support CONFIRMED the G1 mic is NOT a developer interface: only the built-in ASR (https://support.unitree.com/home/en/G1_developer/VuiClient_Service), or an external USB-C mic/speaker array for custom speech. Exhaustively reproduced on the EDU: ASR api 1002 -> 3104; the only ALSA capture node held exclusively by the factory; wake-word capture not on the XBAR mux; no rt/audiosender republish; no on-board ASR log. Wake-up Conversation Mode streams the mic off-robot to the vendor app/cloud.

asr.path: human as a Device Connect agent (Bluetooth headset + local whisper -> human_agent), or an external USB-C mic array + local ASR

walk_policy.source: unitree_rl_lab velocity-walk (exported ONNX, Isaac-Lab-native); run as a torch MLP on GPU (no onnxruntime GPU on aarch64). See deploy-policy + locomotion.
walk_policy.obs_spec: 480-D = 5-step term-major history of [base_ang_vel*0.2 (3), projected_gravity (3), velocity_commands (3), joint_pos_rel (29), joint_vel_rel*0.05 (29), last_action (29)].
walk_policy.action_spec: raw*0.25 + default_joint_pos, applied to the 29 body joints in Isaac articulation order. On the 23-DOF robot the 6 absent motor slots are no-ops (see sim_real_reconciliation.locked_sim_joints).

hand_substitution.rationale: Match FINGER COUNT, not brand: a 5-finger sim hand preserves the grasp morphology of the real Brainco hand. The Isaac G1 default Dex3 (3-finger) was rejected for this reason. The fingers are held at their default during a reach in either case — the body policy controls only the 23/29 body joints, never the fingers — so the Brainco↔Inspire difference does not enter the trained policy.

sim_real_reconciliation.notes: These 6 sim joints exist in the 29-DOF asset but are ABSENT on this 23-DOF robot — the Unitree SDK enum marks them 'INVALID for g1 23dof' (WaistRoll/WaistPitch and L/R WristPitch/WristYaw). For a transfer-valid policy, lock them at default and EXCLUDE them from the action set so the policy reaches/balances using only the 12 legs + waist_yaw + 10 arm joints the hardware actually has. CAVEAT: the issue#2 bed-reach policy was trained on the FULL 29-DOF (it leans using waist pitch the 23-DOF robot lacks), so it is a valid SIM result but NOT directly transfer-valid to this robot — retrain with these joints locked for hardware transfer.

provenance.characterized_by: discover-robot (Claude Code), over SSH to the live G1 EDU; effector DOF read from g1_23dof_mode_10.urdf + the unitree_sdk2_python G1JointIndex enum; sensors from the robotics-connect *_sight on-hardware calibrations.
provenance.notes: HARDWARE-SOURCED on the live robot: the 23-DOF body + joint set (23 revolute joints in g1_23dof_mode_10.urdf), the waist being yaw-only and the wrists roll-only (the SDK enum marks the other 6 'INVALID for g1 23dof'), the camera down-tilt constant (CAMERA_TILT_DEG_DEFAULT=51.29 on-disk), the sensors/hand on USB (RealSense D435i + Brainco-via-FT4232H quad), and the 'pd_gains' = the FACTORY Kp/Kd that ship with the robot, read from unitree_sdk2_python/example/g1/low_level/g1_low_level_example.py (legs Kp 60/60/60/100/40/40 Kd 1/1/1/2/1/1; waist Kp 60/40/40; arms Kp 40 Kd 1). NOTE on gains: these factory low-level gains are the position-control deploy gains; the armwaheed/robots#2 whole-body RL balance policy was STAGED with the stiffer unitree_rl_gym walk-policy training gains (hips kp100/knee150/ankle40/waist200/arms40, Kd 2/4/2/5/10) — pick per the deploy controller (factory gains for low-level control, walk-policy gains to match the RL walk). 'default_pose' is the velocity-walk policy's neutral (the walk default the reach policy is centred on). The 29-DOF sim joint_order is the Isaac articulation order used by the velocity-walk deploy.
